findings.design_reference: FR-010 / SC-002 / NFR-007; security-compliance and test-integrity lenses; prior finding f3 from 20260711T211335918-97a93603
findings.comment: HUMAN DECISION REQUIRED: stop the autonomous review/fix loop. Prior blocking finding f3 remains unresolved and has no worktree-visible human deferral. The orchestrator still runs declared verification directly in the reviewer worktree at line 776 and then invokes the reviewer on that same worktree at line 806 without restoring or rematerializing it when source_mutated is true. The production-path test at tests/continuous-co-review/unit/orchestrator-verification-injection.Tests.ps1:183-198 explicitly accepts this unsafe behavior: it tampers with app.txt and proves only that mutation is recorded before the reviewer mock runs, not that reviewer inputs are preserved. A mutating declaration can therefore alter implementation or review context before inspection. A human must decide the containment-preserving resolution (disposable child copy, or abort plus certified-tree rematerialization/restoration) before further autonomous rounds.
